title: Enchanted Forge: Silk Dyeing Workshop
description: The user operates a mystical silk dyeing workshop where a pot of glowing coals heats magical dye pigments. The user must drag the SpecularSilkPouf through colored energy fields emanating from the PotOfCoals to dye it in layers, adjusting heat intensity, rotation angle, and immersion depth to achieve a target color pattern across multiple dyeing passes.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive silk dyeing workshop scene. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader: place PotOfCoals at the center-bottom of the scene on a stone platform (a dark gray cylinder), and SpecularSilkPouf floating above it. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 6) looking at origin with OrbitControls for viewing. Add ambient light (intensity 0.3), a warm point light inside the coals (orange, intensity 2, with distance falloff), and a hemisphere light (sky blue/ground brown). Create a visible particle system of 200 small spheres around the pot representing rising colored energy (cycle through red, blue, gold pigments). Implement these interactions requiring parameter validation: (1) A heat slider (range 100-900°C) that changes the point light intensity and particle speed proportionally, validated to stay in range. (2) Click-and-drag on the SpecularSilkPouf to lower it toward the coals — track Y position, clamping between 0.5 and 4.0 units. (3) A rotation knob (0-360°) controlling pouf spin speed during immersion for even coating. (4) A pigment selector with three buttons (Crimson, Sapphire, Gold) that changes particle color and the tint applied to the pouf material. (5) An 'Immerse' button that triggers a 3-second animation lowering the pouf into the energy field, applying the selected color tint to the pouf's material (modify its MeshStandardMaterial color, blending with existing color). (6) A dye intensity slider (0.1-1.0) controlling how strongly each pass tints the material. (7) Display a HUD showing current temperature, selected pigment, dye count (max 5 passes), and resulting RGB hex value. Validate that temperature must be above 300°C before immersion is allowed. After 5 passes, show a completion banner with the final color hex code. Use emissive properties on the coals mesh to make them glow based on temperature.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  heatTemperature: number,         // current forge temperature in Celsius (100-900)
  poufYPosition: number,         // vertical position of silk pouf in scene units (0.5-4.0)
  rotationAngle: number,         // current rotation angle of pouf in degrees (0-360)
  selectedPigment: string,         // active dye pigment name (Crimson|Sapphire|Gold)
  particleSpeed: number,         // velocity multiplier for rising energy particles (0.5-3.0)
  lightIntensity: number,         // point light intensity inside coals (0.5-4.0)
  immersionDepth: number,         // how deep pouf is in energy field, derived from Y position (0.0-1.0)
  isDragging: boolean,         // whether user is currently dragging the pouf
  dyeLayerCount: number,         // accumulated dye layers applied to pouf (0-10)
  coatingUniformity: number,         // evenness of dye distribution based on rotation (0.0-1.0)
  particleCount: number,         // number of active energy particles in scene (200)
}
